# Photograph Description

This sepia-toned photograph shows a horse-drawn delivery wagon parked on a city street in front of a storefront. A white horse is harnessed to pull a wooden cart with large spoked wheels. Two people are visible on the wagon: a woman seated in the cart and a man standing behind, wearing a light-colored shirt and cap. The storefront behind displays signage reading "BOTTLERS SUPPLIES" and "Real FLAVOR & EXTRACT," with additional partial text visible including "MILK" and what appears to be product names like "ORANGE ADE" and "SARATOGA." The storefront has large windows and the building appears to be several stories tall. A vintage automobile is partially visible in the background to the right. The photograph appears to be from the early 20th century and has yellowed with age, with visible wear and damage to the edges of the paper mount.
Collection name: Jesse Brown Cook Scrapbooks Documenting San Francisco History and Law Enforcement

# Image Description

This is a stereoscopic card from the Keystone View Company, a manufacturer of stereoscope pictures, numbered 58 and labeled 21084. The card contains two nearly identical sepia-toned photographs side by side, designed to create a three-dimensional effect when viewed through a stereoscope. The images show a woman in early 20th-century clothing (long dress and hat) hand-feeding ice cream to a small black bear cub that stands upright on its hind legs. The scene takes place outdoors in what appears to be an enclosure, with a wire mesh fence visible in the background, brick buildings, and foliage visible behind the fence. The caption at the bottom reads: "Mrs. Herbert Hoover Feeds Ice Cream to a Small Black Bear Cub, Seward, Alaska." A grayscale tone strip for color reference appears at the top of the card, and text along the left edge identifies this as part of the "Keystone View Company Miscellaneous Builders" collection.

# Description

This black and white photograph captures an urban street scene showing extensive destruction and reconstruction. On the left side stands an intact neoclassical building with a prominent domed roof featuring a latticed design, classical columns supporting a portico, and ornamental architectural details. In sharp contrast, the surrounding area is filled with rubble, debris, and scattered building materials covering the street and ground. Damaged and partially destroyed brick buildings with collapsed walls and exposed structures line the right side of the image and extend into the background. Electrical wires and trolley lines cross overhead. Construction equipment, wooden scaffolding, and a canvas covering are visible among the debris. A few figures can be seen walking or working in the street. A horse-drawn cart is positioned on the right side of the scene. The photograph appears to document post-war or disaster-related urban reconstruction, with the preserved classical building standing as a stark contrast to the devastation surrounding it. At the bottom of the image is a color reference scale used for archival purposes.
Collection name: 1906 San Francisco Earthquake and Fire Digital Collection
